title: Project Kennel Trust Manifest
description: Cryptographic boundaries and execution triggers for host-side tooling. A confined Project Kennel workload cannot see or rewrite this file (it is masked inside the kennel), so a host IDE can trust its pins to detect workspace-trigger tampering (T2.8).

generator.description: The tool that generated or last updated this manifest.

execution.description: Defines what host tooling is allowed to execute.

triggers.description: Key-value pairs mapping relative file paths to their expected SHA-256 hashes. Host tools must refuse to execute these files if the hash mismatches.

^.*$.description: A lowercase hex-encoded SHA-256 hash, prefixed with 'sha256:'.

boundaries.description: Negative trust spaces. Host tools must treat these paths as no-exec.

untrusted_paths.description: List of relative paths or globs that are strictly untrusted.
